blockers: Extracted headless modules were not registered in .largefile-manifest.json.
Snapshot evidence publish failure could leave an initial snapshot/history write behind.
Dedicated loop/continuation regression evidence was insufficient.
runHeadlessCliMainOrchestrator still exposed publisher-returned workflow_state on snapshot publish failure.
Dirty workflow_state failure-path regression evidence was missing.
notes: The reviewer also conflated .largefile-manifest.json registration with runtime run-manifest events; the second delta prompt corrected that terminology.

closed_blockers.evidence: The five extracted headless modules are registered as accepted extraction modules in .largefile-manifest.json, all below 300 lines.
src/workflow/headless-snapshot-publisher.js rolls back the initial snapshot and history file when evidence publish fails; test/headless-cli-loop-continuation.test.js covers rollback.

src/workflow/headless-cli-orchestrator.js returns the pre-publish workflowState on snapshot publish failure; test/headless-cli-loop-continuation.test.js verifies publisher-mutated workflow state is not exposed.
test/headless-cli-loop-continuation.test.js covers two bounded loop iterations, must_continue propagation, next_action continuity, snapshot persistence, history latest, and cycle_id progression.

non_blocking: Some local helper duplication remains across extracted modules, but no shard treated it as blocking.
runHeadlessCliMainOrchestrator and runHeadlessCliMainOrchestratorLoop remain future extraction targets under the new 900-line target.
